text: What are you looking to buy?

options.label: ICT Software or SaaS
options.description: Cloud software, subscriptions, platforms, apps

options.label: ICT Hardware or devices
options.description: Computers, servers, networking equipment, peripherals

options.label: Professional services
options.description: Specialist delivery — legal, engineering, accounting, IT delivery

options.label: Consulting or advisory
options.description: Strategic advice, research, policy, strategy

options.label: Goods or supplies
options.description: Physical products, equipment, consumables, stationery

options.label: Construction or built environment
options.description: Building works, fit-out, maintenance, infrastructure

options.label: Labour or contingent workforce
options.description: Temporary staff, contractors, labour hire

options.label: Something else
options.description: Not sure, or doesn't fit the categories above

text: What is the main purpose of this purchase?

options.label: New capability or service
options.description: Something we don't currently have

options.label: Renewing or extending an existing contract
options.description: Continuing what we already have in place

options.label: Replacing something that's ending or failed
options.description: An existing system, contract, or arrangement is expiring or no longer working

options.label: Pilot or proof of concept
options.description: Testing an idea before committing to full delivery

options.label: Emergency or urgent operational need
options.description: Something has gone wrong and we need to act quickly

text: How clearly do you know what you need?
hint: Think about whether you could hand someone a spec or statement of work today.

options.label: Very clear — I have a detailed spec or SOW ready
options.description: Requirements are defined and documented

options.label: Mostly clear — I know what I need but some detail is missing
options.description: Core requirement is understood; some gaps to fill

options.label: Partially defined — I need market input to refine it
options.description: I have a broad idea but need to understand what's possible

options.label: Early stage — I'm exploring options and don't have a firm requirement yet
options.description: The problem is understood but the solution isn't defined

text: Do you have a budget or estimate for this?
hint: This helps identify the right process and approvals. Give your best estimate — it doesn't need to be exact.

options.label: Under $10,000
options.description: Petty cash / low-value purchase
Low-value procurement
Medium-value procurement
High-value procurement

options.label: Over $1,000,000
options.description: Major procurement

options.label: Not sure yet
options.description: Budget hasn't been confirmed

text: Where do you sit in the organisation?
hint: This helps with delegation and approval routing.

options.label: Frontline or operational team
options.description: Delivering services, programs, or operations directly

options.label: Corporate or enabling function
options.description: Finance, HR, legal, IT, communications, etc.

options.label: Executive or senior leadership
options.description: Deputy Secretary level and above

options.label: Central or shared services agency
options.description: Agency that provides services to other agencies

text: How many suppliers do you think could realistically provide this?
hint: Think about who you'd find if you searched the market today.

options.label: Only one that I know of
options.description: Sole supplier or highly specialised capability

options.label: 2 to 3 specialist providers
options.description: Very limited market, niche capability

options.label: Several suppliers — maybe 4 to 10
options.description: Some competition but not a commodity market

options.label: Many suppliers available
options.description: Competitive market, multiple credible options

options.label: Not sure
options.description: Haven't assessed the market yet

text: If this goes wrong, what is the likely impact?
hint: Think about the worst realistic outcome — not the absolute worst case.

options.label: Minor inconvenience — easily managed
options.description: Low disruption, no significant consequence

options.label: Operational disruption — noticeable but recoverable
options.description: Some service disruption or cost to the agency

options.label: Significant service impact — affects many people or programs
options.description: Major disruption, reputational or financial consequences

options.label: Critical failure — safety risk, major legal exposure, or catastrophic disruption
options.description: Could cause serious harm, critical system failure, or major public consequences

text: Does this procurement involve any of the following?
hint: Select all that apply. These trigger additional obligations and review requirements.

options.label: AI or automated decision-making
options.description: Systems that use AI, machine learning, or automated decisions affecting people

options.label: Personal or health information
options.description: Data about individuals, health records, or sensitive personal data

options.label: Critical ICT infrastructure
options.description: Core systems, networks, or platforms that government depends on

options.label: Construction or built environment work
options.description: Physical building, infrastructure, or fit-out

options.label: Overseas supply chains
options.description: Products or services sourced or manufactured overseas

options.label: Small or medium enterprise (SME) supplier
options.description: The intended or likely supplier is an SME — fewer than 200 employees

options.label: None of the above
options.description: No special overlays apply

text: What level of supplier interaction will this require?
hint: Think about how you'll need to engage suppliers — this helps shape the process.

options.label: Buy off-the-shelf — no negotiation needed
options.description: Standard product or service, just purchase it

options.label: Get a few quotes and compare
options.description: Request pricing from a small number of suppliers

options.label: Run a formal tender or competitive process
options.description: Invite and evaluate formal submissions

options.label: Collaborate with suppliers to define or co-design the solution
options.description: The requirement will evolve through supplier engagement

text: What is your timeframe for this procurement?

options.label: I need it within 2 weeks
options.description: Urgent — very compressed timeline

options.label: 2 to 8 weeks
options.description: Compressed but workable with the right process

options.label: 2 to 6 months
options.description: Normal procurement timeline

options.label: 6 months or more
options.description: Extended — time to do this thoroughly

options.label: Not yet determined
options.description: Timeline hasn't been set

text: Does this procurement involve AI, machine learning, or automated decision-making?
hint: Includes AI-embedded products, predictive analytics, chatbots, or algorithmic decision tools.

options.label: Yes
options.description: AI, ML, or automated decision-making is a component

options.label: No
options.description: No AI or automated decision-making component

options.label: Not sure
options.description: Unsure if AI is involved

text: How sensitive is the data this system will handle?
hint: Consider what personal, commercial, or government information the system will access or store.

options.label: Public data only
options.description: No personal or sensitive information

options.label: Internal / business information
options.description: Internal govt documents, commercial data, not personal

options.label: Personal information
options.description: Names, contacts, identifiers, or personal details of individuals

options.label: Sensitive or protected data
options.description: Health, financial, law enforcement, or classified information

text: How critical is this system or service to service delivery?
hint: Consider the impact if the system failed or was unavailable for more than 48 hours.

options.label: Can be deferred or has workarounds
options.description: Operations can continue if this is unavailable

options.label: Important but not mission-critical
options.description: Disruption would be felt but manageable

options.label: Critical to service delivery
options.description: Failure would significantly impact frontline services

options.label: Mission-critical / non-negotiable
options.description: Failure would halt essential government services
